current_wind.description: Moderate to strong wind from the South-Southwest.

wind_info.description: A 7 o'clock wind from your left, slightly behind you.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 7 o'clock wind from your left pushing gently from behind, your RHBH will naturally fight it. Focus on a clean hyzer release with a stable to overstable driver for Hole 1 (Yellow, 413ft) to keep it straight and prevent an unintentional cut-roll into the OB to your left.

fh.shot_strategy: For Hole 1 (Black, 447ft), the 7 o'clock wind from the left is manageable for an RHFH. Throw a more overstable driver on a clean hyzer to control its flight path and avoid the OB left, ensuring a predictable fade towards the basket.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, with the 7 o'clock tailwind from your left on Hole 1 (Yellow, 413ft), an LHBH will drift right. Use an understable driver and a smooth hyzer to get forward distance, letting the wind carry it towards the center of the fairway and avoiding the OB to the left.

fh.shot_strategy: For Hole 1 (Black, 447ft), the 7 o'clock tailwind from your left can be a challenge for an LHFH. A stable driver thrown with a controlled anhyzer will fight the wind and fade back, aiming to stay away from the OB on the left side of the fairway.

wind_info.description: A strong 10 o'clock crosswind from the right.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, Hole A (210ft) has a significant 10 o'clock crosswind from your right. Your RHBH flight plate will be exposed, so opt for an overstable midrange or fairway driver thrown with a hyzer to fight the wind and prevent it from blowing your disc right into the OB or into the fairway cross.

fh.shot_strategy: To tackle Hole A (210ft) with the strong 10 o'clock crosswind from the right, an RHFH with an overstable fairway driver is your best bet. Focus on a clean release with a flat to hyzer angle to counter the wind's push and keep the disc on a controlled path towards the basket.

bh.shot_strategy: For Hole A (210ft), the 10 o'clock crosswind from the right will push your LHBH shot even further right. Use an understable midrange and a smooth anhyzer to get the disc to hold its line and fade back towards the basket, avoiding the strong rightward push into the OB.

fh.shot_strategy: Player, the 10 o'clock crosswind from your right on Hole A (210ft) means an LHFH will need significant hyzer to compensate. Throw an overstable fairway driver with a strong hyzer angle to fight the wind's push and ensure your shot lands safely away from the OB on the right.

wind_info.description: A 5 o'clock wind from your right, pushing slightly away.

bh.shot_strategy: On Hole 2 (Yellow, 259ft), the 5 o'clock wind from your right will help your RHBH glide. Throw a stable driver with a touch of hyzer to control its flight and avoid the OB area that stretches to your right, guiding it towards the center of the fairway.

fh.shot_strategy: For Hole 2 (Black, 350ft), the 5 o'clock wind from your right can assist an RHFH. Use an overstable driver and a flat release to manage the wind's push and ensure your shot doesn't drift too far right into the OB, aiming for a controlled flight towards the basket.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, Hole 2 (Yellow, 259ft) has a 5 o'clock wind from your right. An LHBH will have its natural fade fighting this wind. Use an understable driver on a hyzer flip to get maximum distance and keep it from fading out too early, while staying clear of the OB to your right.

fh.shot_strategy: With a 5 o'clock wind from your right on Hole 2 (Black, 350ft), an LHFH will be pushed further right. A stable driver thrown with a controlled anhyzer should work well, fighting the wind and fading back towards the basket, but be mindful of the OB to the right.
